summary: The app-layout-navigation feature has been successfully implemented according to all specified requirements. The Header component includes logo/app name linking to /dashboard, nav links (Dashboard, New Plan, Playbooks), user email display, and signout button. Navigation links correctly highlight the active route using usePathname. On mobile (375px), a hamburger menu provides accessible navigation. A custom 404 not-found page with back to dashboard link is created. All protected routes use the layout via the (protected) route group. All 41 tests pass, TypeScript compiles without errors, and linting is clean.
sharedStateObservations: Header component uses 'use client' directive with useState and usePathname hooks - this pattern is working well for client-side navigation state.

The active route highlighting uses a helper function isActive() that handles special cases like sub-routes for playbooks (/playbooks/*) - this pattern could be documented for future nav link implementations.
Mobile menu accessibility is well-implemented with aria-expanded, aria-label, and role attributes - good pattern to maintain.
Protected route group (protected) layout.tsx correctly checks auth and redirects unauthenticated users - this is a clean Next.js 15 pattern for route-level auth.
The header.test.tsx contains 14 comprehensive tests covering all major functionality - good test coverage model for other components.

commandsRun.observation: All 14 header component tests passing: logo rendering, nav links, active route highlighting for all routes including sub-routes, user email display, sign out button, mobile menu toggle, and mobile menu content
All 4 not-found page tests passing: 404 heading, page not found message, explanatory text, and back to dashboard link
No ESLint warnings or errors (aside from deprecation notice for next lint command itself)

interactiveChecks.observed: Protected layout correctly wraps children with Header component, passes user email to Header, and redirects unauthenticated users to /auth/signin

Header component implements: CareerAC logo linking to /dashboard, nav links for Dashboard/New Plan/Playbooks, user email display (when provided), signout button form, mobile hamburger menu with toggle functionality, active route highlighting using usePathname, proper accessibility attributes (aria-expanded, aria-label, role=navigation)
Custom 404 page with 404 heading, 'Page not found' message, explanatory text, and 'Back to Dashboard' link to /dashboard with proper styling
Stub pages created within (protected) route group so nav links navigate correctly to existing routes
